programme.title: The Agenda
programme.desc: A weekly talk-show looking beyond the headlines and “accepted wisdom” to find the real story. Hosted by award-winning journalist and presenter Stephen Cole, The Agenda quite simply sets The Agenda . You won’t find the same-old talking heads recycling platitudes; instead thought-provoking guests to debate and explore key issues of today’s world. Using virtual reality graphics to bring subjects to life, Stephen will navigate through subjects ranging from “Prove That Climate Change Exists” to “Who Will Win the Race to Space”. Opposing views will be given opportunity to flourish and develop into a meaningful conversation. The Agenda: No nonsense talk; Challenging perceptions.

programme.title: Razor
programme.desc: CGTN's brand new science and technology show, covering cutting edge developments from all over the world. RAZOR will show you the world's most powerful laser and the world's smallest anti-cancer treatment. It will take you from the weird world of quantum computing to the boundaries of the cosmos. An international team of reporters will follow the guidance of Occam's Razor - the principle that the simplest explanation is the best one. RAZOR will show that we are already ""living in the future"!

programme.title: Best Eats
programme.desc: Food connects people! A feast for the eyes that spotlights the cuisines of China in a way that’s bound to set the mouths of the whole globe watering!

programme.title: Youthful China
programme.desc: The youth of a country is the future of that country. A series of documentaries focusing on the younger generation of China in which you get to see what they are achieving and what makes them tick!

programme.title: Pixel Collage
programme.desc: A well-crafted collage of selected short documentaries telling stories about all aspects of China! People, technology, culture, and wildlife, all are showcased in Internet-friendly form.

programme.title: Today’s Top Pick
programme.desc: Documentaries in this slot are rebroadcasts of our most-viewed and most popular stories of the season. They feature important social, economic, environmental or other policy-driven issues, affecting lives nationwide or simply in one small village. A broad selection of top productions by Chinese top documentary producers.

programme.title: Humanities and Geography
programme.desc: A strand devoted to the nature and wildlife of China, the people who lived alongside them, and the great and diverse landscapes with which they all contend.

programme.title: Living China
programme.desc: Real life stories told from the personal perspective. Moving, dramatic, sorrows and joys, expressed emphatically and expressed quietly. Watch closely to see and understand the real China as it is lived.

programme.title: Special Edition
programme.desc: Prestige Chinese documentary production, tackling serious subjects with the best cinematography, editing, and all round production values with a unique Chinese perspective.

programme.title: Journeys and Discoveries
programme.desc: A documentary series showcasing the history of China and its people.

programme.desc: A documentary series featuring historical and archaeological exploration.

programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.desc: Es un programa nuevo del canal, de corte noticioso con estilo de revista, y que se transmite una vez a la semana. Aquí ofrecemos reportajes y análisis en profundidad sobre los temas más debatidos y de mayor actualidad en la zona americana, y en especial en América Latina.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: ASÍ ES CHINA
programme.desc: Descubre para el televidente el gran legado cultural e histórico de las distintas regiones y etnias de China. También le acerca a la vida y las costumbres de la gente común y le muestra la evolución del país en aspectos como educación, sociedad y protección del medio ambiente y del patrimonio histórico y natural.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: AMÉRICA AHORA
programme.desc: Es un programa nuevo del canal, de corte noticioso con estilo de revista, y que se transmite una vez a la semana. Aquí ofrecemos reportajes y análisis en profundidad sobre los temas más debatidos y de mayor actualidad en la zona americana, y en especial en América Latina.

programme.title: DÍALOGO
programme.desc: Convoca a celebridades, eruditos y funcionarios gubernamentales tanto de China como del exterior, para debatir temas de palpitante actualidad e interés general y de proyección internacional.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: CGTN NOTICIAS
programme.desc: Es el informativo estelar del canal, con las últimas noticias del acontecer político, social, deportivo y cultural de China y el resto del mundo. Los hechos que constituyen titulares están aquí, en CGTN Noticias.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: MIRADA ECONÓMICA

programme.title: PRISMA
programme.desc: Prisma es un programa cultural producido por CGTN en español en colaboración con el multimedio de comunicación latinoamericano teleSUR. Su objetivo es mostrar a los televidentes la riqueza cultural de China y el mundo hispanohablante, elevar el conocimiento de la cultura china en los países de habla hispana y establecer una plataforma de diálogo entre ambas partes. Se trata de un programa mensual de 30 minutos de duración que comenzó a emitirse en agosto de 2016. Está compuesto de tres segmentos que destacan por su diversidad temática. Primero, un espacio de noticias culturales; después, una semblanza de alguna personalidad del ámbito cultural latinoamericano; y finalmente un documental sobre aspectos de la cultura china.

programme.title: RONDA ARTÍSTICA
programme.desc: Se exhibe la diversidad de cantos y danzas, óperas locales tradicionales, música y artes folklóricas de China.

programme.title: HORA INFANTIL
programme.desc: Una selección de las mejores series chinas de dibujos animados, dobladas al español. Hora Infantil es una ventana a través de la cual los niños y adolescentes de los países de habla hispana pueden llegar a comprender mejor qué es y cómo es China.

programme.title: CAFÉ DE CGTN
programme.desc: En este espacio, los compañeros de la familia de CGTN en español, intercambian opiniones y debaten de forma casual los temas económicos, sociales y culturales de mayor actualidad en China.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: EL PROYECTOR
programme.desc: Acérquese a la vida de la China antigua y moderna a través de las producciones dramáticas de CGTN-Español. La combinación perfecta de historia y ficción chinas en la pantalla de su televisor.

programme.title: SABOREANDO CHINA
programme.desc: Aprenda a preparar las recetas más populares de China. Nuestro chef le revelará de forma práctica los secretos del arte culinario de esta nación asiática. Conozca los ingredientes más utilizados en la variada cocina regional, los orígenes de los platos e incluso sus beneficios para la salud. En De China a tu cocina, el espectador podrá experimentar a la vez la riqueza tradicional y la variedad gastronómica de China.

programme.title: NIHAO CHINA
programme.desc: Es la clase de chino de CGTN-Español. Las series “Aprendamos juntos pinyin”, “Comunícate en chino” y “Viajando y aprendiendo chino”, abordan diferentes niveles de aprendizaje de la nueva lengua de moda en el mundo, brindando así a los televidentes la posibilidad de ampliar su vocabulario, mejorar su gramática y dominar las frases y expresiones más usuales del idioma con más hablantes en el planeta.

programme.title: DOCUMENTAL
programme.desc: Presentapersonajes y acontecimientos típicos, avances y descubrimientos tecnológicos y científicos, y la civilización social de China.

programme.title: Summer Davos 2026: The Future of Jobs, Skills, and Sustainable Growth
programme.desc:
Global trade is undergoing profound change as economies grapple with shifting supply chains, rapid advances in artificial intelligence, and the transition to cleaner energy.
We are coming to you from the Summer Davos Forum in Dalian, where policymakers, business leaders, and academics from around the world are discussing how to navigate these transformations.
One key question at this year's forum is how these changes will affect workers, and whether they will create new opportunities or deepen existing challenges. To discuss the future of jobs, skills, and sustainable growth, Song Ruixin is joined by Professor Mercedes Maroto-Valer, Deputy Principal for Global Sustainability at Heriot-Watt University, and Kathleen Alexander, co-founder and CEO of Savor, a San Jose based food startup.

programme.title: Meet China: From Little Toys to Global Factories, How Chinese Creativity Travels
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we explore how “Made in China” is taking on new forms. A monster toy becomes a global collectible, an ocean park in southern China turns wonder into repeat visits, and a smart EV factory in Zhejiang shows how cars are built for overseas markets. In central China’s Xuchang, a small city supplies much of the world’s hair products, while in Guangxi, an Italian chef blends pizza with traditional medicinal food. From blind boxes to electric vehicles, wigs and a yam-and-lamb pizza, this episode reveals how Chinese creativity, scale and culture are reaching the world in unexpected ways.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: Beyond the screen: how “film ” tourism and AI redefine moviegoer experience
programme.desc: As the Beijing International Film Festival takes over the city, films are no longer just something we watch in cinemas. They’re connecting tourism, retail, entertainment, and even cutting-edge tech. What does this “film plus” experience look like for audiences today? And as AI reshapes the film industry, what opportunities and challenges lie ahead? Host Xu Yawen spoke with Li Lun, assistant professor of economics at Peking University; Geng Ling, film producer and CEO of China Film Assist Company; and Teng Jimeng, associate professor of American studies at Beijing Foreign Studies University, to delve into these questions.

programme.title: What can Africa learn from China’s poverty reduction experience?
programme.desc: Five years after China eliminated absolute poverty, its development experience continues to attract attention around the world, particularly across Africa. What lessons can African countries draw from China’s journey? Can China’s poverty reduction experiences be adapted to African realities? In today’s episode, we bring scholars, journalists and development practitioners from China, The Gambia, Rwanda and Namibia. Jainaba Sonko, Zhang Yanqiu, Patrick Rurangangabo and Jeronimo Kateya explore pathways to sustainable development and poverty reduction, discussing governance, infrastructure, education, self-reliance and the future of China-Africa cooperation.

programme.title: China's Capital Markets H2 Outlook
programme.desc:
As global investors reassess China’s outlook, the country’s capital markets are back in focus. With international investment banks raising their growth forecasts and overseas capital flowing back in, the question is no longer whether investors are coming back – but what will drive the next phase of growth.
Join BizTalk this week, as Wang Dan, China director at Eurasia Group, and Laura Wang, chief China equity strategist at Morgan Stanley, discuss the outlook for China’s equity and bond markets.

programme.title: Meet China: Turning Villages into Stages, Taking Creativity to the World
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we step into the frontlines of rural revitalization to see how a Gen-Z village chief is breathing new life into her hometown. We then visit the world’s toy capital, where Chinese manufacturing is turning culture and creativity into global stories. From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: The World Cup Economy
programme.desc: As the FIFA World Cup kicks off, the spotlight is not only on the field, but also on the massive economic opportunities unfolding beyond the stadiums. From tourism to business partnerships, the world's top sporting event is bringing people, markets and ideas closer together. But what does it take to turn global exposure into long-term growth? And what are the new trends shaping sports marketing? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the business opportunities behind "the beautiful game."

programme.title: China Agenda 2026
programme.desc: Special coverage of China's annual sessions of its top legislature and political advisory body, known as the "Two Sessions".

programme.title: China's concert boom: Why Gen Z is filling the stadiums
programme.desc:
From packed stadium shows to sold-out performances in smaller cities, China's live music scene is expanding rapidly. Young audiences are turning concerts into a cultural routine: traveling across cities, sharing the experience online and building collective memories around live performances.
The boom now extends far beyond the stage, boosting tourism, retail spending and local economies. In this episode of Unboxing China, we explore how concerts are reshaping consumption patterns and becoming a new driver of cultural and urban development.

As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: China Agenda 2026
programme.desc: Special coverage of China's annual sessions of its top legislature and political advisory body, known as the "Two Sessions".

programme.title: China's concert boom: Why Gen Z is filling the stadiums
programme.desc:
From packed stadium shows to sold-out performances in smaller cities, China's live music scene is expanding rapidly. Young audiences are turning concerts into a cultural routine: traveling across cities, sharing the experience online and building collective memories around live performances.
The boom now extends far beyond the stage, boosting tourism, retail spending and local economies. In this episode of Unboxing China, we explore how concerts are reshaping consumption patterns and becoming a new driver of cultural and urban development.

programme.title: China's Capital Markets H2 Outlook
programme.desc:
As global investors reassess China’s outlook, the country’s capital markets are back in focus. With international investment banks raising their growth forecasts and overseas capital flowing back in, the question is no longer whether investors are coming back – but what will drive the next phase of growth.
Join BizTalk this week, as Wang Dan, China director at Eurasia Group, and Laura Wang, chief China equity strategist at Morgan Stanley, discuss the outlook for China’s equity and bond markets.

programme.title: Meet China: Turning Villages into Stages, Taking Creativity to the World
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we step into the frontlines of rural revitalization to see how a Gen-Z village chief is breathing new life into her hometown. We then visit the world’s toy capital, where Chinese manufacturing is turning culture and creativity into global stories. From a green beef cycle near Beijing to rice-prawn co-culture in Cambodia, this episode explores how technology, industry and cooperation are helping land thrive and livelihoods grow.

programme.title: Football expert: AI will never replace referees or players
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence (AI) is playing an increasingly important role at the 2026 World Cup. However, Chris Van Puyvelde, Chinese Football Association Technical Director, said that AI as a tool will never replace players or referees, as the human brain is the ultimate decision-maker in football, in a #Bizpodcast show with CGTN. #BizFocus

programme.title: China Capital Market H1: Review and Outlook
programme.desc: International investors' confidence towards China continues to rise with foreign capital steadily flowing into its equity and bond markets. BizTalk will speak to guests from Morgan Stanley and Eurasia Group on Tuesday, June 30th – the last trading day of the first half of the year – to analyze the reason behind accelerating allocation of Chinese stocks and bonds by foreign financial institutions.

programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Global Business-Asian Edition Ep.2
programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Meet China: From Little Toys to Global Factories, How Chinese Creativity Travels
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we explore how “Made in China” is taking on new forms. A monster toy becomes a global collectible, an ocean park in southern China turns wonder into repeat visits, and a smart EV factory in Zhejiang shows how cars are built for overseas markets. In central China’s Xuchang, a small city supplies much of the world’s hair products, while in Guangxi, an Italian chef blends pizza with traditional medicinal food. From blind boxes to electric vehicles, wigs and a yam-and-lamb pizza, this episode reveals how Chinese creativity, scale and culture are reaching the world in unexpected ways.

programme.title: BizTalk | Summer Davos special: New opportunities for growth in China
programme.desc:
The 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions, also known as Summer Davos, is underway in Dalian. During the forum, China’s technological innovation, green development and business environment have once again become key topics of discussion.
In this special episode of BizTalk, CGTN speaks with Liu Zhenmin, China’s special envoy for climate change, Joe Ngai, chairman of McKinsey Greater China, and Leo Lee, president of Novartis China, to explore how China is cultivating new growth drivers amid a new wave of industrial transformation, and why “the next China is still China”.

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: China's rural CEO experiment: How a Dai village is reinventing itself
programme.desc:
During the Dai New Year, CGTN reporter Lincoln Humphries visited Manluanzhan, a traditional Dai village near China's southwestern border in Yunnan Province.
There, "sleeping" assets are being awakened: an abandoned water tower has been turned into a café, and traditional Dai houses have been expanded to offer new types of homestays.
At the center of this experiment is an unusual figure — a rural CEO who helps balance economic development with the Dai community's traditions as the village charts its own path forward.
In this episode of #BizFocus, Lincoln Humphries shows how a village in Xishuangbanna brings fresh vitality and authentic traditions together.

programme.title: The new keeper of the ancient village in the deep mountains
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains of Fujian, Yanxiang village is home to centuries-old Hakka architecture and an aging population. But a post 1995-born returnee is giving the ancient village a new future. Follow CGTN’s He Jingyi to see how a young village chief blends modern ideas with traditional heritage, breathing new life into this millennium-old rural settlement. #BizFocus

programme.title: How rafting helped one village find new life
programme.desc: In Sankengtan Village, Guangdong Province, a river that once flowed quietly through everyday life is now part of the Gulong Gorge rafting course—a global destination drawing millions of visitors each year. What was once a quiet rural community has been transformed by tourism, as local residents build new livelihoods around the river and the opportunities it brings. In this episode of BizFocus, CGTN's Vivienne Nunis joins those braving the white water to find out more.

programme.desc: Global Business is the flagship business show of CGTN English Channel, bringing you the latest economic data, business trends and policies.

programme.title: Meet China: From Little Toys to Global Factories, How Chinese Creativity Travels
programme.desc: In this episode of Meet China, we explore how “Made in China” is taking on new forms. A monster toy becomes a global collectible, an ocean park in southern China turns wonder into repeat visits, and a smart EV factory in Zhejiang shows how cars are built for overseas markets. In central China’s Xuchang, a small city supplies much of the world’s hair products, while in Guangxi, an Italian chef blends pizza with traditional medicinal food. From blind boxes to electric vehicles, wigs and a yam-and-lamb pizza, this episode reveals how Chinese creativity, scale and culture are reaching the world in unexpected ways.

programme.title: BizTalk | Summer Davos special: New opportunities for growth in China
programme.desc:
The 17th Annual Meeting of the New Champions, also known as Summer Davos, is underway in Dalian. During the forum, China’s technological innovation, green development and business environment have once again become key topics of discussion.
In this special episode of BizTalk, CGTN speaks with Liu Zhenmin, China’s special envoy for climate change, Joe Ngai, chairman of McKinsey Greater China, and Leo Lee, president of Novartis China, to explore how China is cultivating new growth drivers amid a new wave of industrial transformation, and why “the next China is still China”.

programme.title: Biztalk Special Coverage on AI EP3
programme.desc: The Token Economy Demand for AI tokens is surging, with trends like “tokenmaxxing” accelerating the race for computing power. As pressure mounts on computing and energy infrastructure, China is leveraging its clean energy strengths to support AI growth, while “token exports” emerge as a new way to deliver AI services to global users. What’s the future like for the “token economy”? Join us on BizTalk as we explore the economics behind the AI revolution.

programme.title: China's rural CEO experiment: How a Dai village is reinventing itself
programme.desc:
During the Dai New Year, CGTN reporter Lincoln Humphries visited Manluanzhan, a traditional Dai village near China's southwestern border in Yunnan Province.
There, "sleeping" assets are being awakened: an abandoned water tower has been turned into a café, and traditional Dai houses have been expanded to offer new types of homestays.
At the center of this experiment is an unusual figure — a rural CEO who helps balance economic development with the Dai community's traditions as the village charts its own path forward.
In this episode of #BizFocus, Lincoln Humphries shows how a village in Xishuangbanna brings fresh vitality and authentic traditions together.

programme.title: The new keeper of the ancient village in the deep mountains
programme.desc: Deep in the mountains of Fujian, Yanxiang village is home to centuries-old Hakka architecture and an aging population. But a post 1995-born returnee is giving the ancient village a new future. Follow CGTN’s He Jingyi to see how a young village chief blends modern ideas with traditional heritage, breathing new life into this millennium-old rural settlement. #BizFocus

programme.title: How rafting helped one village find new life
programme.desc: In Sankengtan Village, Guangdong Province, a river that once flowed quietly through everyday life is now part of the Gulong Gorge rafting course—a global destination drawing millions of visitors each year. What was once a quiet rural community has been transformed by tourism, as local residents build new livelihoods around the river and the opportunities it brings. In this episode of BizFocus, CGTN's Vivienne Nunis joins those braving the white water to find out more.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Foodwise: Wuzhen
programme.desc: Join our hosts Tianran He and Eva May as they explore the boisterous event of Wuzhen's International Theatre festival, with colorful features of both theatrical and culinary nature. Foodie Tianran and art-lover Eva are accompanied by ubiquitous local street food, daily carnival performances and meet and greets with fascinating personalities. Let's set the stage for the beautiful merge of food and drama as they taste their way through the charming scene of Wuzhen.

programme.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.2
programme.desc: Sichuan's western mountains are a shield safeguarding a true national treasure: the Chengdu Plain, China's very own land of plenty. Join us as we dive from high plateaus into deep gorges, scale snow peaks, and follow rivers in pursuit of an engineering marvel bequeathed by the ancients...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.2
programme.desc: Sichuan's western mountains are a shield safeguarding a true national treasure: the Chengdu Plain, China's very own land of plenty. Join us as we dive from high plateaus into deep gorges, scale snow peaks, and follow rivers in pursuit of an engineering marvel bequeathed by the ancients...

programme.title: Shared Roots in Wrestling
programme.desc: The documentary “Shared Roots in Wrestling” follows a young wrestler from Tianjin as he journeys to Inner Mongolia to explore the shared heritage of Chinese-style wrestling and Bökh, the region’s national wrestling tradition. Part of CGTN’s original series “Next Stop: China,” this episode uncovers how strength, skill, and resilience connect athletes across city streets and endless grasslands, carrying forward a legacy that transcends geography and generations.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programme.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the "king of brocades." It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as "weaving through the warp and cutting the weft"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programme.title: China Untapped: Huzhou
programme.desc: Located in the east of China, on the south shore of Taihu Lake, Huzhou has been well-known for its richness in resources and blooming in silk trade since the ancient times. Guided by the concept of "lucid waters and lush mountains are invaluable assets", Huzhou now is committed to developing green industries. In this episode, CGTN hosts Tianran He and Hannah Wilson travelled to Huzhou, where they tried a variety of guesthouse hotels in the Moganshan Mountains; experienced the life of digital nomads in Anji’s countryside; learned about the eco-concepts behind China's traditional architectures in Nanxun Ancient Town; and explored the development of eco-agriculture powered by high-tech along the Taihu Lake. All these phenomena demonstrate that economic development and environmental protection can achieve harmonious coexistence, and that civilization thrives if ecology is prosperous.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council. The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been influenced by the area’s close proximity to the sea.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.1
programme.desc: Lhasa, set high on the “Roof of the World” in China’s southwest, is both the capital of Xizang Autonomous Region, and the spiritual heart of Tibetan culture. In this episode, host Tianran He ventures deep into the fabled Potala Palace, discovering ancient meditation caves, priceless relics and golden rooftops. Then, he tries his hand at Tibetan papermaking, learning how a poisonous ingredient is crucial for making paper that can survive for centuries. Finally, Tianran dons his apron and learns to make iconic Tibetan dishes, including one said to reveal your fortune!

programme.title: An Encounter with China’s Endangered Monkeys
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve in Diqing Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, to learn about and participate in the conservation work for the rare and precious national treasure: the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to conduct research alongside young volunteers. Through these activities, we recognize the importance of environmental education for young people in promoting natural ecological balance and harmonious development.

programme.title: Foodwise: Quanzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in Quanzhou, the ancient starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. From its covered arcades and Roman columns to its Southeast Asian vibe, our hosts discover that Quanzhou truly is a melting pot of world cultures. Then there’s the food: From the local noodle porridge that utilizes Silk Road spices, to fried vinegar pork and a dessert made from a special algae, there’s plenty of unique dishes to try here. Over a cup of oolong, our hosts will discover how Quanzhou’s tea changed the world, and how trade with Arab civilizations influenced a local fishing village. So join us in Quanzhou, a city that UNESCO designated as an “Emporium of the World”!

programme.title: Foodwise: Xi'an
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Xi'an, an ancient city with over 3000 years of history, capital to 13 Chinese dynasties. But first, our hosts are going to carb load on street food, from glutinous rice cakes and cakes made out of persimmons, to mutton soup with flatbread and the famous biangbiang noodles. They'll also taste the rich history of Xi'an by experiencing a Qin Dynasty-inspired banquet, and try millennia-old dishes such as trout grilled on a ceramic tile and calabash chicken, a Tang Dynasty delicacy. From the Terracotta Army to the Daming Palace, residence of the Tang emperors, to a dazzling nighttime boulevard that recreates the grandeur of the Tang Dynasty, there's so much to explore in Xi'an. So, get your camera and your stomach ready for CGTN's Foodwise in Xi'an!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Aerial China II-2 Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.3
programme.desc: Inner Mongolia spans China from east to west. Little wonder then, that it's such a land of diversity - epic landscapes of forests, grasslands and deserts, Inner Mongolia has it all!

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: From Stone to Story
programme.desc: China's profound stone carving culture dates back to the Han Dynasty (202 BC – 220 AD) and flourished during the Tang Dynasty (618 – 907 AD), evolving into a distinctive art form and unique cultural practice. Among many places famous for stone carving, Hui’an County—through generations of dedicated artisans—has risen to prominence and earned the esteemed title of "World Capital of Stone Carving," awarded by the World Crafts Council. The county’s skilled stonemasons are celebrated for their ability to transform ordinary pieces of rock into beautiful works of art. This episode shines a spotlight on the intangible cultural heritage of Hui’an stone carving and reveals how it has been influenced by the area’s close proximity to the sea.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.1
programme.desc: Lhasa, set high on the “Roof of the World” in China’s southwest, is both the capital of Xizang Autonomous Region, and the spiritual heart of Tibetan culture. In this episode, host Tianran He ventures deep into the fabled Potala Palace, discovering ancient meditation caves, priceless relics and golden rooftops. Then, he tries his hand at Tibetan papermaking, learning how a poisonous ingredient is crucial for making paper that can survive for centuries. Finally, Tianran dons his apron and learns to make iconic Tibetan dishes, including one said to reveal your fortune!

programme.title: An Encounter with China’s Endangered Monkeys
programme.desc: In this episode, we head to Baima Snow Mountain Nature Reserve in Diqing Tibetan Autonomous Prefecture, Yunnan Province, to learn about and participate in the conservation work for the rare and precious national treasure: the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. The presenter ventures into the forest, experiences the daily life of local forest rangers, and witnesses the harmonious relationship between humans and monkeys. We interview "the Monkey Father"—Long Yongcheng, chief scientist of The Nature Conservancy (TNC) and leader of China's Primate Expert Group—who shares stories about the two decades of efforts dedicated to protecting the Yunnan snub-nosed monkey. We also go to the mountains to conduct research alongside young volunteers. Through these activities, we recognize the importance of environmental education for young people in promoting natural ecological balance and harmonious development.

programme.title: Foodwise: Quanzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in Quanzhou, the ancient starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. From its covered arcades and Roman columns to its Southeast Asian vibe, our hosts discover that Quanzhou truly is a melting pot of world cultures. Then there’s the food: From the local noodle porridge that utilizes Silk Road spices, to fried vinegar pork and a dessert made from a special algae, there’s plenty of unique dishes to try here. Over a cup of oolong, our hosts will discover how Quanzhou’s tea changed the world, and how trade with Arab civilizations influenced a local fishing village. So join us in Quanzhou, a city that UNESCO designated as an “Emporium of the World”!

programme.title: Foodwise: Xi'an
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Xi'an, an ancient city with over 3000 years of history, capital to 13 Chinese dynasties. But first, our hosts are going to carb load on street food, from glutinous rice cakes and cakes made out of persimmons, to mutton soup with flatbread and the famous biangbiang noodles. They'll also taste the rich history of Xi'an by experiencing a Qin Dynasty-inspired banquet, and try millennia-old dishes such as trout grilled on a ceramic tile and calabash chicken, a Tang Dynasty delicacy. From the Terracotta Army to the Daming Palace, residence of the Tang emperors, to a dazzling nighttime boulevard that recreates the grandeur of the Tang Dynasty, there's so much to explore in Xi'an. So, get your camera and your stomach ready for CGTN's Foodwise in Xi'an!

programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the " Vehicle on the Plateau". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: The sound of casting
programme.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.2
programme.desc: Tianran He explores Lhasa’s bustling Barkhor Street, where pilgrims, locals, and visitors alike circle its ancient lanes. This multicultural district, home to sacred temples and boutique shops, becomes the backdrop as Tianran dons traditional Tibetan attire for a photoshoot. Across the city, he meets high school students dancing for Douyin — China’s version of TikTok, blending street dance with traditional Tibetan moves. Finally, Tianran travels to the birthplace of Tibetan incense, where waterwheels still power this thousand-year-old craft.

programme.title: The Great Wall Homestay
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran He's heading to the mountains on Beijing’s northern outskirts, to meet some people his age who have swapped city life for the chance to run guesthouses beside the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Datong
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Angela Fu are in historic Datong. Located in northern China's Shanxi Province, some 1500 years ago Datong was the capital of the Northern Wei Dynasty, which saw nomads unite northern China and Buddhism spread throughout the country. At the renowned Yungang Grottoes where giant Buddhas were carved out of cliffs, our hosts discover Datong's multicultural past and sample the emperor's favorite cake. They seek out the remains of a Jesuit church beneath the Great Wall of China, and find in the local cuisine elements that echo the past, and modern-day recipes for thousand-year old dishes.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chaozhou
programme.desc: In this episode, hosts Tianran He and Angela Fu are in Chaozhou, South China. They're on a quest to discover the secrets of its success, for Chaozhou is famed for its wealthy merchant guilds and its homegrown Teochew cuisine, which enjoys global recognition. From working as part-timers in a beef hotpot restaurant, to learning about tea ceremony and shadowing a master of Teochew cuisine, our hosts discover the best way to learn about Chaozhou, is through its food.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.4
programme.desc: Gansu, in China's northwest, is a land dominated by tremendous forces of nature: mountain ranges flanking north and south press to form the ancient Hexi Corridor, and, of course, the mighty Yellow River. Taking off in central Gansu, gliding over farmlands past and present, behold the Loess Plateau's hidden beauty as we go on a quest for an ancient city called Dunhuang...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.4
programme.desc: Gansu, in China's northwest, is a land dominated by tremendous forces of nature: mountain ranges flanking north and south press to form the ancient Hexi Corridor, and, of course, the mighty Yellow River. Taking off in central Gansu, gliding over farmlands past and present, behold the Loess Plateau's hidden beauty as we go on a quest for an ancient city called Dunhuang...

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-6 Shandong Mantis Style
programme.desc: The final episode traces the cross-ocean transmission of Shandong Mantis Style. In the mountains of northern Italy, host Kleber visits Dante Basili, a veteran Italian practitioner in his sixties, who recounts travelling to China 36 years ago to learn Mantis Style and returning home to teach Chinese martial arts ever since. Kleber then heads to Shandong to meet Yu Bo and Yu Tao, sons of Mantis Style grandmaster Yu Hai. He witnesses a traditional apprenticeship ceremony and explores the style’s fighting essence: adopting its spirit rather than merely copying its form. The episode shows how Mantis Style has taken root and flourished in different cultural settings. Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Five Ancestors Fist
programme.desc: In Quanzhou, southeast China, Kleber seeks out masters of the Five Ancestors Fist, a combination of five local kungfu styles. He meets the abbot of a Southern Shaolin Temple, and takes part in the extraordinary training regime used by Shaolin monks to hone their bodies and minds. He experiences the intricacies of Quanzhou’s traditional puppetry, and learns how Five Ancestors Fist incorporated military tactics that allowed its practitioners to band together and fight off pirates.

programme.title: The sound of casting
programme.desc: For centuries, the fires of craftsmanship have never faded in Pingyao. In this UNESCO World Heritage city once famed for trade, three ancient metalworking traditions — sword casting, gold and silver crafting, and iron forging — continue to thrive. This episode steps into the workshops where artisans shape metal with fire and devotion. Each blade’s edge, each ornament’s gleam, and each curve of wrought iron tells a story of resilience and artistry — revealing how the spirit of Pingyao’s craftsmanship still glows in the heart of time.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.2
programme.desc: Tianran He explores Lhasa’s bustling Barkhor Street, where pilgrims, locals, and visitors alike circle its ancient lanes. This multicultural district, home to sacred temples and boutique shops, becomes the backdrop as Tianran dons traditional Tibetan attire for a photoshoot. Across the city, he meets high school students dancing for Douyin — China’s version of TikTok, blending street dance with traditional Tibetan moves. Finally, Tianran travels to the birthplace of Tibetan incense, where waterwheels still power this thousand-year-old craft.

programme.title: The Great Wall Homestay
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran He's heading to the mountains on Beijing’s northern outskirts, to meet some people his age who have swapped city life for the chance to run guesthouses beside the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Datong
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Angela Fu are in historic Datong. Located in northern China's Shanxi Province, some 1500 years ago Datong was the capital of the Northern Wei Dynasty, which saw nomads unite northern China and Buddhism spread throughout the country. At the renowned Yungang Grottoes where giant Buddhas were carved out of cliffs, our hosts discover Datong's multicultural past and sample the emperor's favorite cake. They seek out the remains of a Jesuit church beneath the Great Wall of China, and find in the local cuisine elements that echo the past, and modern-day recipes for thousand-year old dishes.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chaozhou
programme.desc: In this episode, hosts Tianran He and Angela Fu are in Chaozhou, South China. They're on a quest to discover the secrets of its success, for Chaozhou is famed for its wealthy merchant guilds and its homegrown Teochew cuisine, which enjoys global recognition. From working as part-timers in a beef hotpot restaurant, to learning about tea ceremony and shadowing a master of Teochew cuisine, our hosts discover the best way to learn about Chaozhou, is through its food.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.4
programme.desc: Gansu, in China's northwest, is a land dominated by tremendous forces of nature: mountain ranges flanking north and south press to form the ancient Hexi Corridor, and, of course, the mighty Yellow River. Taking off in central Gansu, gliding over farmlands past and present, behold the Loess Plateau's hidden beauty as we go on a quest for an ancient city called Dunhuang...

Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.4
programme.desc: Gansu, in China's northwest, is a land dominated by tremendous forces of nature: mountain ranges flanking north and south press to form the ancient Hexi Corridor, and, of course, the mighty Yellow River. Taking off in central Gansu, gliding over farmlands past and present, behold the Loess Plateau's hidden beauty as we go on a quest for an ancient city called Dunhuang...

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of Tibetan antelopes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-6 Shandong Mantis Style
programme.desc: The final episode traces the cross-ocean transmission of Shandong Mantis Style. In the mountains of northern Italy, host Kleber visits Dante Basili, a veteran Italian practitioner in his sixties, who recounts travelling to China 36 years ago to learn Mantis Style and returning home to teach Chinese martial arts ever since. Kleber then heads to Shandong to meet Yu Bo and Yu Tao, sons of Mantis Style grandmaster Yu Hai. He witnesses a traditional apprenticeship ceremony and explores the style’s fighting essence: adopting its spirit rather than merely copying its form. The episode shows how Mantis Style has taken root and flourished in different cultural settings. Ultimately, this journey across Europe and Asia suggests that transmission is not only about techniques, but also about dialogue across time and distance, and a shared resonance of spirit between cultures.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: White Crane
programme.desc: In Yongchun, China, Kleber explores the origins of White Crane kungfu and its effect on the martial arts world. He learns how its blocks and strikes were inspired by animal movements, and how acupuncture points can be used to deadly effect. After training his grip and finger strength with wooden sticks, he travels to Beijing for an introduction to Goju-ryu karate, and discovers striking similarities with White Crane.

programme.title: Miao Embroidery: History in Stitches
programme.desc: According to legend, during a migration by the Miao ethnic group, a female chieftain wanted to keep a record of all the mountains and lakes she passed along the way. The means she chose of doing so was by stitching the images on her dress. By the time they reached a suitable place to settle, her clothing was covered with embroidered designs. This, it’s said, was the origin of Miao embroidery. As the story tells us, the designs in Miao embroidery are closely related to the natural world and daily life. The Miao people have a special fondness for nature. So, as well as depicting their daily farming and work activities, they copy birds, flowers, mountains, and grass onto their clothing. Over the years, the embroidery of the Miao people has become a written record of their life. The stitches are like characters which, when many of them are combined, become a record of their history, their collected wisdom, and the knowledge passed down from their ancestors.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.3
programme.desc: Join Tianran He on a high-speed train to Shigatse, gateway to the Himalayas and home to Mount Qomolangma, the world’s highest peak. As the city with the most land ports in Xizang, Shigatse serves as a key corridor to South Asia. Here, Tianran samples breakfast at a bustling local teahouse and experiences monastic life at the vast Tashilunpo Monastery. He then visits an agricultural park where tropical crops thrive against the odds of the alpine plateau, before discovering pulu — a traditional Tibetan textile now earning global acclaim.

programme.title: A Village Homestay in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, our presenter Tianran He’s heading out of Beijing proper, to the city’s Miyun District. There he’ll meet some people running guesthouses who gave up their day jobs, in order to achieve the dream of a place of their own in the country.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of golden snub-nosed monkeys
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.5
programme.desc: Guangdong, sheltered by the Nanling Mountains, is a province where rivers cascade from hills on their way to the ocean. But before they meet the sea, they pass through another land of plenty, a place where solid earth encounters the rolling waves in a truly loving embrace - the Pearl River Delta.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.5
programme.desc: Guangdong, sheltered by the Nanling Mountains, is a province where rivers cascade from hills on their way to the ocean. But before they meet the sea, they pass through another land of plenty, a place where solid earth encounters the rolling waves in a truly loving embrace - the Pearl River Delta.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of golden snub-nosed monkeys
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise. Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake Style. First he visits the Ip Man lineage, based on the legendary kungfu master whose life story spawned hundreds of martial arts movies, with Bruce Lee counting among his students. With Ip Man Wing Chun, Kleber learns how to create powerful short strikes perfectly suited to fighting in Foshan’s narrow alleys. With Snake Style, he experiences unique training methods that give its practitioners superhuman flexibility, allowing them to stick to their opponents, and even fight on boats in canals

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

This, it’s said, was the origin of Miao embroidery. As the story tells us, the designs in Miao embroidery are closely related to the natural world and daily life. The Miao people have a special fondness for nature. So, as well as depicting their daily farming and work activities, they copy birds, flowers, mountains, and grass onto their clothing. Over the years, the embroidery of the Miao people has become a written record of their life. The stitches are like characters which, when many of them are combined, become a record of their history, their collected wisdom, and the knowledge passed down from their ancestors.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.3
programme.desc: Join Tianran He on a high-speed train to Shigatse, gateway to the Himalayas and home to Mount Qomolangma, the world’s highest peak. As the city with the most land ports in Xizang, Shigatse serves as a key corridor to South Asia. Here, Tianran samples breakfast at a bustling local teahouse and experiences monastic life at the vast Tashilunpo Monastery. He then visits an agricultural park where tropical crops thrive against the odds of the alpine plateau, before discovering pulu — a traditional Tibetan textile now earning global acclaim.

programme.title: A Village Homestay in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, our presenter Tianran He’s heading out of Beijing proper, to the city’s Miyun District. There he’ll meet some people running guesthouses who gave up their day jobs, in order to achieve the dream of a place of their own in the country.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of golden snub-nosed monkeys
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.5
programme.desc: Guangdong, sheltered by the Nanling Mountains, is a province where rivers cascade from hills on their way to the ocean. But before they meet the sea, they pass through another land of plenty, a place where solid earth encounters the rolling waves in a truly loving embrace - the Pearl River Delta.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.5
programme.desc: Guangdong, sheltered by the Nanling Mountains, is a province where rivers cascade from hills on their way to the ocean. But before they meet the sea, they pass through another land of plenty, a place where solid earth encounters the rolling waves in a truly loving embrace - the Pearl River Delta.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of golden snub-nosed monkeys
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Wing Chun
programme.desc: Martial arts lover Kleber Battaglia is on a mission: To seek out China's kungfu masters, and learn their secrets. From explosive Wing Chun to fluid Taiji Quan, slippery Snake Style to elegant White Crane, he'll learn hidden moves and ancient techniques for defeating powerful opponents. But ?there’s more to kungfu than combat: From culture and philosophy, to wellness and insights from the natural world, Kleber will discover that contained within every kick, strike and throw, is the condensed wisdom of the ancient masters. This is Kungfu-wise. Kleber travels to Foshan, the birthplace of Wing Chun. A practitioner of this style, he seeks out masters of two different lineages: Ip Man and Snake Style. First he visits the Ip Man lineage, based on the legendary kungfu master whose life story spawned hundreds of martial arts movies, with Bruce Lee counting among his students. With Ip Man Wing Chun, Kleber learns how to create powerful short strikes perfectly suited to fighting in Foshan’s narrow alleys. With Snake Style, he experiences unique training methods that give its practitioners superhuman flexibility, allowing them to stick to their opponents, and even fight on boats in canals

programme.title: Outkirts of Wenzhou
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He explores Wenzhou’s counties. From geological wonders and daring cliffside stunts at the Yandang Mountains, to rafting along the scenic Nanxi River, to harvesting seaweed and shellfish on the mud flats of the Dongtou Islands, Tianran discovers the ingenious ways the people of Wenzhou have transformed the challenges of living between mountains and seas into their greatest strengths.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-1 Baji Quan
programme.desc: In the first episode of Season 2 of Kung Fu Wise, Baji Quan, host Kleber travels to Mengcun, Cangzhou, in Hebei Province. There, he meets Wu Hao, the young head of the Wu-style Baji Quan school. Together, they journey across the North China Plain, from bustling village markets and the family training hall to the Bohai Sea coast, as Kleber experiences Baji Quan’s explosive power first-hand. Along the way, the episode unpacks the idea of “opening the gate”, the key to focused power, and the spirit of spear work. It goes beyond technique to reveal Cangzhou’s rich martial heritage, tracing how Wu-style Baji Quan has travelled from its hometown to Europe, proof that traditional kung fu can endure, evolve, and thrive today. In the end, Wu Hao answers with action: true inheritance is not only about skills, but about shaping spirit and character.

programme.title: Miao Silverware: A Shining Epic
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the fascinating world of the silver-working art of the Miao ethnic group. Join us, and hear the stories and see the works of four remarkable individuals. From a national-level inheritor who is preserving the traditional craft, to an artist infusing personal flair into his designs; from a female silversmith imbuing her work with feminine warmth, to a young artisan inspiring a new generation, we investigate the rich heritage, cultural significance, and evolving potential of Miao silverware.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He travels to Shannan, the cradle of Tibetan culture. Nestled in the gentlest valley of the Yarlung Zangbo River, this is where the first Tibetan king rose to power, the first palace in Xizang was built, and many other “firsts” in Xizang’s history began. Tianran visits Yamdrok Lake, one of Xizang’s three sacred lakes, where a paragliding base is taking shape. He then learns the art of Tibetan opera, joins rangers on a mission to track the elusive Tibetan red deer, and ends the day with a rejuvenating soak in a village revitalized by hot spring tourism.

programme.title: Fun Guide to China: Hainan 1
programme.desc: As Hainan moves towards implementing its special customs operation, CGTN’s Travelogue is launching a brand-new series, “Fun Guide to China”. Set in Hainan, the pilot episode journeys through its cities, mountains, coastlines and rainforests, capturing the vibrant atmosphere of life in the Free Trade Port.From the world’s largest stand-alone duty-free mall – a true shopper’s paradise, to the laid-back charm of an “Old Dad Tea” restaurant, host Tianran He explores the evolving cityscape of Haikou. On the northeast coast in Wenchang, he joins a local photographer to witness the launch of a Chinese space rocket, before diving into interactive space museums that bring the cosmos within reach. The journey then heads west to Danzhou, where campervans and “mermaids” share the same stage, before reaching Wanning on the east coast, where Tianran catches waves at one of China’s most famous surfing hotspots.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of black-necked cranes
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.6
programme.desc: The mountainous realm of Fujian's centers around the Wuyi range, just opposite Taiwan Province. Join us as we trace a heritage of tea production in ancient villages, marvel at waterside Danxia, and discover terraces where seas of lotuses grow...

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.6
programme.desc: The mountainous realm of Fujian's centers around the Wuyi range, just opposite Taiwan Province. Join us as we trace a heritage of tea production in ancient villages, marvel at waterside Danxia, and discover terraces where seas of lotuses grow...
