programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Wenzhou
programme.desc: Tianran He is in Wenzhou, southeast China — a city famed for its self-made millionaires. Here he meets the first Chinese shoemaker to expand overseas, and hears the stories of other local entrepreneurs. Along the way, he samples traditional snacks, visits some of China’s oldest lighthouses, and enjoys an evening of Ou Opera. He also learns the craft of papermaking, before heading into the hills to soak in hot springs and wander across ancient covered bridges.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.5
programme.desc: In this final episode, Tianran journeys to Nyingchi, known as “Xizang’s Garden.” Here, he experiences the region’s semi-pastoral way of life — feeding yaks, trying his hand at archery, and seeing how tourism is transforming lives in Lulang International Tourist Town. Travelling along National Highway 318, Tianran discovers towering millennia-old cypress trees and rafts through the vast Yani National Wetland Park, before ending the day with a unique spa treatment at a traditional Tibetan hospital.

programme.title: Fun Guide to China: Hainan 2
programme.desc: In this episode host Tianran He continues his adventure across Hainan. This time, the road trip begins in Dongfang, where Tianran tucks into roast suckling pig by day and, by night, attends grassroots basketball games that rival NBA games in atmosphere. In the mountains of Wuzhishan, Tianran explores a lush jungle with forest rangers, before stepping back in time to 1988 for a retro-chic disco night. In Lingshui, the journey moves offshore as Tianran enjoys a seafood feast on a floating fishing platform, then heads back to shore to visit a contemporary art museum by the pier. After reaching Sanya in the south, he joins scuba divers on a mission to restore coral reefs, before winding down on a yacht with friends and laughter. Finally, Tianran travels to Baisha for the “La’aomen” harvest festival, where the local ethnic Li people sing, dance and celebrate their most important crop: “Mountain Orchid Rice”.

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of milu deer
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.

programme.title: Foodwise: The Herbal Way of Life
programme.desc: 'Medicine tonic is not as good as food tonic' - this Chinese saying is a core principle of Traditional Chinese Medicine. It is believed that the right diet and nourishment can cure and reverse the effects of disease, hence considered an inevitable source of natural health preservation. In this Foodwise episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May explore this ancient concept of medicine food homology as they taste their way through different herbs, chat to experts of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and visit venues that combine conventional principles with modern ideas. Dive deep into the origin of TCM with us as we learn about the harmony between man and nature and its application in modern functional foods!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.7
programme.desc: Jiangsu---an aquatic lowland stitched together by the Yangtze and Grand Canal. From Yellow Sea wetlands populated by red-crowned cranes to the Yangtze estuary's port history, this is a land and coast where maritime legends are made!

programme.title: Aerial China II Ep.7
programme.desc: Jiangsu---an aquatic lowland stitched together by the Yangtze and Grand Canal. From Yellow Sea wetlands populated by red-crowned cranes to the Yangtze estuary's port history, this is a land and coast where maritime legends are made!

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of milu deer
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Exploring Ethnic Attire: Dong Elegance
programme.desc: Dong clothing weaves together tradition, craftsmanship, and the unique identity of the Dong ethnic group. In this episode, we’ll unravel the stories behind the intricate designs, vibrant colors, and deep cultural significance of Dong attire. From ceremonial garments to everyday wear, each piece is imbued with the essence of a rich heritage.

programme.desc: Jiangsu---an aquatic lowland stitched together by the Yangtze and Grand Canal. From Yellow Sea wetlands populated by red-crowned cranes to the Yangtze estuary's port history, this is a land and coast where maritime legends are made!

programme.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of milu deer
programme.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Crossroads of Cultures: The Legacy of Silk Roads
programme.desc: Quanzhou, situated on the southeastern coast of China, once served as a maritime hub of the East and Southeast Asia trade network during the 10th to 14th centuries, gaining prominence for its exceptional trading port. With 22 distinctive heritage sites listed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, this city stands as a testament to the legendary history of the Silk Roads. In this program, we invite four individuals living in Quanzhou to showcase their daily lives, capturing their journeys through the city's world heritage sites. Through their perspectives, we present a dynamic portrayal of a city rich in heritage. From the maritime splendor of Quanzhou's history to the serene echoes of its ancient city, from the historical treasures of the Luoyang Bridge to the vibrancy of the modern urban landscape, we journey alongside our guests through time and space, exploring Quanzhou's diverse charm and unique allure. Join us as we immerse ourselves in a city that bridges history and cultures, and embraces innovation.

programme.title: China Bound: Guangzhou
programme.desc: Join us in Guangzhou, a global manufacturing hub in south China with a population as large as New York’s. From browsing hip street markets to wholesale clothes shopping, sampling world-famous Cantonese cuisine, and visiting the childhood home of kungfu legend Bruce Lee, there is plenty to do during our stay.

programme.title: China Untapped: Xizang Ep.3
programme.desc: Join Tianran He on a high-speed train to Shigatse, gateway to the Himalayas and home to Mount Qomolangma, the world’s highest peak. As the city with the most land ports in Xizang, Shigatse serves as a key corridor to South Asia. Here, Tianran samples breakfast at a bustling local teahouse and experiences monastic life at the vast Tashilunpo Monastery. He then visits an agricultural park where tropical crops thrive against the odds of the alpine plateau, before discovering pulu — a traditional Tibetan textile now earning global acclaim.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing
programme.desc: From the local delicacies of Beijing's Ox Street to latest hangouts and contemporary cuisine of the Chinese capital, hosts Tianran He and Eva May will give you a true taste of Beijing, and the fascinating stories and people behind them.

programme.title: Foodwise: Beijing's Hutongs
programme.desc: The Hutongs in Beijing are considered the city's blood vessels, as these traditional alleyways make up the DNA of the capital for its rich history and culture. In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May zigzag their way through the Hutongs and the Forbidden City for a mouth-watering experience full of ancient and modern delicacies. Starring German designer Katrin, Russian artist Liuba and photographer Feng Yu from Beijing, our hosts are taking you along a journey that features fried noodles, dim sum, pancakes and co.!

programme.title: A Village Homestay in Beijing
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, our presenter Tianran He’s heading out of Beijing proper, to the city’s Miyun District. There he’ll meet some people running guesthouses who gave up their day jobs, in order to achieve the dream of a place of their own in the country.

programme.title: Diabolo
programme.desc: In China, the diabolo has a history of over 600 years. From the earliest uniaxial design, it developed to have a double axis. With time, it came to be made in various shapes and from various materials. And its popularity began to spread around the world. In the hands of an expert, a diabolo can create a truly mesmerizing spectacle. So, let’s hear from a professional about the various tricks of his trade.

programme.title: The Spirit of Zhaxidele
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran backpacks the way to the western reaches of China. Making his home in beautiful Jiaju village, he learns just what it takes to be a Tibetan, then reaches the mysterious village of Suopo and explore its ancient watchtowers.

programme.title: Young drummer of Ansai
programme.desc: Soaring leaps and the energetic beats of the Ansai waist drum dance made Gao Yuchen, a nine-year-old boy, fall in love with the dance at first sight. Growing up in Ansai, Shaanxi, Gao was first drawn to the large-scale folk dance while watching a local performance. As a form of intangible cultural heritage, the Ansai waist drum dance was promoted by local authorities and was included as part of his school's PE program. Gao was able to receive waist drum dance training as an extracurricular activity at his school, and he subsequently became a rising star on campus. He says he wants to become a better dancer and hopes to perform on bigger stages.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. programme.title: Secrets of the Rainforest Ep.1
programme.desc: Hainan’s tropical rainforest boasts stunning beauty and rich biodiversity, underscoring the importance of conservation.

programme.title: History Speaks 2
programme.desc: During World War II, Japanese militarism continued to expand outward, bringing deep suffering to the nations of East Asia. As time passes, more and more witnesses to history are passing away. Meanwhile, right-wing forces within the Japanese government have attempted to downplay or even cover up this history of aggression, making the tough but necessary task of preserving the memory of the past more urgent than ever.

programme.title: Encounters Along the Silk Road Ep.1

programme.title: The Lush Green Gannan Ep.2
programme.desc: This documentary captures the remarkable transformation of Gannan over the past decade, a region that serves as a critical water source and recharge zone for the upper reaches of the Yellow and Yangtze rivers, a national ecological functional area, and a pilot zone for ecological civilization.

programme.title: The Lush Green Gannan Ep.3

programme.title: Song Dynasty Poetry: Beauty Across Time Ep.4
programme.desc: Through the lens of Song dynasty poetry, this documentary brings to life the era's great poets, their works, and the stories behind them. By delving into their values and worldview, it reveals the deeper spirit within the culture, resulting in a work that is at once thoughtful, artistic, and deeply human.

programme.title: China's Approach to Tackling Climate Change Ep.4
programme.desc: Highlighting China’s role as a major nation, dive into its strategies for energy conservation, emission reduction, ecological restoration, and advancing climate governance.

programme.title: Razor in China·High-speed Train
programme.desc: From steam locomotives to intelligent high-speed rail, the development of China's railways is a story of continuous breakthroughs and innovation. Enter the world's most complex high-speed rail system and discover how China operates the largest high-speed railway network on earth.

programme.title: Back to the Far Side
programme.desc: Hear the stories of the Chang'e-6 engineers and four international payload teams and how they achieved their key mission milestones. Spanning China, France, the Netherlands, Sweden, Italy, and Pakistan, it offers a global perspective on humanity's first-ever mission to bring back samples from the far side of the moon.

programme.title: General Guan Yu Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary is a unique exploration of the late Eastern Han dynasty general Guan Yu. Through a gaming team's recreation of the Battle of Xiangfan, overseas collections of his porcelain sculptures, and a coming-of-age ceremony at Guandi temple, this episode recounts his military achievements, while describing a man of loyalty, righteousness, courage, and honor—and an enduring spiritual symbol for the Chinese people.

programme.title: China's Approach to Tackling Climate Change Ep.5
programme.desc: Highlighting China’s role as a major nation, dive into its strategies for energy conservation, emission reduction, ecological restoration, and advancing climate governance.

programme.title: Foodwise: Haikou
programme.desc: Haikou, also known as "Coconut City," is famous for its coconut-infused cuisine. In this episode, Foodwise host Tianran heads to this coastal city to sample coconut chicken hotpot, fruity and spicy soups, silky Hainan pickled noodles, and check out Haikou’s famous local teahouses. Here, food and lifestyle go hand in hand—a perfect reflection of the city's slow, easy rhythm.

programme.title: Foodwise: Hainan's Four Famous Dishes
programme.desc: Follow Foodwise host Tianran as he tracks down Hainan’s four culinary treasures: Wenchang chicken, Jiaji duck, Dongshan goat, and Hele crab. He soon learns what makes these dishes so famous isn’t just their taste, it’s the animals themselves: chickens raised on coconuts, ducks that brave typhoons, cliff-grazing goats and crabs fattened in brackish waters—proving that in Hainan, the best dishes come with a story.

programme.title: Razor in China·Walk Again
programme.desc: This episode documents a groundbreaking surgery that uses technology to help those with spinal cord issues walk again. Hear from patients and doctors as they navigate the long road ahead, and the courage it takes to get there.

programme.title: Razor in China·Humanoids
programme.desc: From top-level design to application, from technical breakthroughs to ecosystem building—humanoid robots are rising at an unprecedented pace in China. But are they really that close to be part of our daily lives? The question is no longer "if," but “when”.

Through a gaming team's recreation of the Battle of Xiangfan, overseas collections of his porcelain sculptures, and a coming-of-age ceremony at Guandi temple, this episode recounts his military achievements, while describing a man of loyalty, righteousness, courage, and honor—and an enduring spiritual symbol for the Chinese people.

programme.title: Foodwise: The Taste of Home
programme.desc: In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva return to their birthplaces—Beijing, China, and Vienna, Austria—to embark on a nostalgic culinary journey, revisiting the flavors of their past while sharing their impressions of their hometowns. From Vienna’s coffee culture to the teahouses in Beijing’s hutongs, every flavor carries a memory, and every meal tells a story of home.

programme.title: Foodwise: Shaoxing
programme.desc: In this episode, Foodwise host Tianran travels to Shaoxing, Zhejiang in search of the tastiest local flavors. From river delicacies and soy-scented cured foods to fermented dishes and the region's legendary yellow wine, Shaoxing’s bold flavors always leave a strong impression— proving that Jiangnan cuisine can be just as assertive as it is refined.

programme.title: Me and My Corps
programme.desc: This documentary offers an in-depth look into the lives of those living and working in the Xinjiang Production and Construction Corps. Divided into four chapters, it tells the stories of ordinary people—capturing their daily lives and the spirit of the era they embody.

programme.title: Yangtze: The River of Life Ep.5
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the current ecological state of the Yangtze River basin—including its fish, wildlife, and plant life—while documenting the efforts behind the Yangtze River protection initiative.

programme.title: Cultural Inheritors Ep.1
programme.desc: From ancient texts to external therapies, from herbal medicine to processing techniques, the documentary Wind in the Apricot Grove explores the world of Traditional Chinese Medicine through eight aspects. programme.title: A Todo Gol
programme.desc: A Todo Gol une fútbol, fe y liderazgo. En el Mundial, invitados comparten análisis y reflexiones sobre valores de Dios fe y Cristo.

programme.title: Ministry Now
programme.desc: Al frente de la nueva normalidad de hoy, la familia Lamb lleva a los espectadores de todo el mundo a Ministry Now. Lleno de fe radical, declaraciones alentadoras y consejos positivos, este programa le ayudará a cambiar su perspectiva hacia la esperanza que Dios aún tiene para su futuro.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: David y Niki Winston nos acompañan para compartir su increíble historia de amor, sanación y segundas oportunidades, ofreciendo ánimo y sabiduría práctica para todos aquellos que necesitan esperanza en sus relaciones.

programme.title: Pastor Sam Rodriguez
programme.desc: Con un mensaje que inspira, eleva y activa el propósito de Dios en cada persona y familia, el pastor Samuel Rodriguez comparte la palabra del señor con enseñanzas, ilustraciones, y verdades que desatan la gloria de Dios en esta generación.

programme.title: Reflections
programme.desc: Disfruta de impresionantes escenas de muchas de las creaciones milagrosas de Dios. Himnos familiares y música original bellamente producida acompañan las Escrituras para ayudarlo a dejarse llevar y rendirse durante un tiempo de oración y meditación diaria

programme.title: Reflections
programme.desc: Disfruta de impresionantes escenas de muchas de las creaciones milagrosas de Dios. Himnos familiares y música original bellamente producida acompañan las Escrituras para ayudarlo a dejarse llevar y rendirse durante un tiempo de oración y meditación diaria

programme.title: A Todo Gol

programme.title: El cielo en la Tierra
programme.desc: Predicación de la Iglesia Cristiana "El Lugar de su Presencia" en Bogotá, Colombia, Latinoamérica. Con temas relevantes y prácticos, aplicables a la vida diaria y consejos bíblicos para alentar, animar y reflexionar en toda situación.

programme.title: El Programa de John Ankerberg
programme.desc: El Programa de John Ankerberg es un ministerio de televisión y medios de comunicación comprometido con la defensa de la fe cristiana, que anima al creyente y desafía al escéptico.

programme.title: Jentezen Franklin
programme.desc: Encuentre respuestas en la Palabra de Dios y aprenda a vivir cada día con un propósito y significado mientras Jentezen Franklin presenta la verdad clara y simple que lo inspirará a ser lo mejor posible.

programme.title: En contacto con Dr. Charles Stanley
programme.desc: Pastor Dr. Charles Stanley usa sus diversos programas y ministerio para difundir el Evangelio a la población del mundo, y utiliza sus predicaciones para convencer a los seguidores del cristianismo a mantener una relación más estrecha con Jesucristo.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: ¿La fe y la ciencia realmente están en conflicto entre sí? ¿O existe evidencia científica que en realidad valida las Escrituras? El director del Wonders Center and Science Museum, David Rives, comparte la notable conexión entre la Biblia y descubrimientos científicos innovadores.

programme.title: Aqua viva con Sergio Hornung
programme.desc: Aqua Viva presenta al Pastor Sergio Hornung cada semana compartiendo un mensaje cargado de fe, vision y herramientas practicas, a fin de experimentar una genuina transformación por el poder del Espíritu Santo.

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimente el poder de Dios en su vida y prepárese para recibir sanidad, restauración, y milagros mientras recibe una palabra llena de unción y poder predicada por el pastor Hernan Castaño desde la Iglesia Ríos De Aceite en Houston, Texas a las naciones. Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!

programme.title: Andrew Wommack La Verdad Del Evangelio
programme.desc: Andrew Wommack comparte cómo la Palabra de Dios transformó su vida. Él cree que el mismo poder puede obrar para ti. Su enseñanza es profunda, clara y tan simple que hasta un niño puede entenderla.

programme.title: Dr. David Jeremiah
programme.desc: El Dr. David Jeremiah, pastor principal de Shadow Mountain Community Church en El Cajon, California, entrega la inmutable Palabra de Dios a un mundo en constante cambio.

programme.title: A Toda Luz
programme.desc: ¿Te hace falta ánimo o quieres crecer en tu fe junto a otros? A Toda Luz es un programa lleno de vida, con conversaciones reales, risas, y verdades que inspiran. Con voces dinámicas y temas relevantes, te anima, te conecta y te acerca más a Dios.

programme.title: Impactando las Naciones
programme.desc: Impactando Naciones es un programa televisivo conducido por la Pastora Wanda Rolón, dedicado a compartir un mensaje transformador de fe, esperanza y poder espiritual y motivando a todo aquel que busca una experiencia real con Dios.

programme.title: En Familia con Luis y Kristen
programme.desc: Escuchar la voz de Dios parecería una de las cosas más difíciles de hacer; ¿Cómo podemos entonar nuestro oído a su Espíritu Santo?

programme.title: El cielo en la Tierra

programme.title: El Programa de John Ankerberg
programme.desc: Los televidentes están invitados a unirse al presentador John Ankerberg mientras presenta una amplia gama de temas espirituales contemporáneos para discusiones en profundidad, a la vez que defiende respuestas bíblicas a algunas de las preguntas más desconsertantes sobre diversos temas.

programme.title: Descubriendo al Jesús Judío
programme.desc: El rabino Schneider explora la obra continua del Espíritu Santo en la vida de cada creyente. Descubre cómo ser más consciente de la presencia de Dios, oírlo con más claridad y caminar cada día en mayor libertad, paz, poder e intimidad con el Señor.

programme.title: Sin Miedo a Nada
programme.desc: El Pastor Iván Delgado te invita a vivir sin fronteras, a derribar obstáculos internos y externos, y a experimentar el poder de la Palabra como fuerza que transforma, sana y dirige el futuro.

programme.title: Sid Roth - ¡Es Sobrenatural!
programme.desc: Steve Kang creció en una familia budista devota, hasta que una sobredosis de drogas adulteradas lo sumió en el infierno. Lo que vio en el infierno, y más tarde en el cielo, revela la verdad sobre la eternidad.

programme.title: Fe y Fuego
programme.desc: Dios nos restaura y unge para que el nombre de Jesús sea famoso. Jesús le dijo a la mujer en el pozo, "tu necesitas una fuente"; un manantial de agua está en constante movimiento por lo tanto no se estanca. Él es nuestra fuente de agua viva.

programme.title: Andrés Spyker de Más Vida
programme.desc: Conéctate cada semana con un mensaje fresco de la palabra de Dios con una aplicación práctica para tu vida.

programme.title: En Familia con Luis y Kristen
programme.desc: Escuchar la voz de Dios parecería una de las cosas más difíciles de hacer; ¿Cómo podemos entonar nuestro oído a su Espíritu Santo?

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimente el poder de Dios en su vida y prepárese para recibir sanidad, restauración, y milagros mientras recibe una palabra llena de unción y poder predicada por el pastor Hernan Castaño desde la Iglesia Ríos De Aceite en Houston, Texas a las naciones. Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!

programme.title: Jason Lozano
programme.desc: La misión de Jason Lozano Ministries es llevar el mensaje de libertad a las naciones. A través de ganar almas y hacer discípulos podemos ayudar a las naciones a hacer una vida de libertad.

programme.title: Descubriendo al Jesús Judío
programme.desc: El Rabino Schneider, un judío creyente en Jesús, enseña pasionalmente las verdades sinérgicas entre la Biblia Hebrea (el Viejo Testamento) y el Nuevo Testamento, que son relevantes y aplicables a la vida real para los televidentes.

programme.title: The Potter's Touch with Bishop T.D. Jakes
programme.desc: Programa de entrevistas diario que aborda temas de actualidad relacionados con la sanidad. Presentado por el obispo TD Jakes.

programme.title: El cielo en la Tierra
programme.desc: Predicación de la Iglesia Cristiana "El Lugar de su Presencia" en Bogotá, Colombia, Latinoamérica. Con temas relevantes y prácticos, aplicables a la vida diaria y consejos bíblicos para alentar, animar y reflexionar en toda situación.
